query: Broccoli, some cashews, and a bunch of mint. Some Indian dish?

queries: Here is a delicious Indian-style broccoli and cashew curry recipe:
Here is a great recipe for Indian broccoli cashew stir-fry with mint chutney:
Here is a fresh mint chutney recipe to pair with an Indian vegetable dish:

recommendation.headline: With broccoli, cashews, and mint on hand, Malai Broccoli lets all three shine in one creamy appetizer.
recommendation.why_it_fits: This is the only recipe on the list that actually calls for mint alongside broccoli and cashews, so you're not left with a stray bunch of herbs. It's built as a rich, marinated appetizer rather than a full gravy dish, which keeps the ingredient list close to what you already have.

missing_items.note: forms the base of the marinade, use a thick plain yogurt
adds the malai richness, half and half works if that's what you have
a grated hard cheese boosts richness but can be skipped
for garnish, mint alone is fine if you don't have it

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Malai Broccoli recipe on mygingergarlickitchen.com from start to finish, it already uses your broccoli, cashews, and mint in the marinade and roasting method.

alternatives.reason: Pick the Shahi Broccoli page from Rumki's Golden Spoon if you'd rather have a saucy curry to eat with rice instead of an appetizer, though you'll set the mint aside.
Choose the broccoli-cashew poriyal from A Bit Wholesomely if you want something quicker and lighter, more of a South Indian stir-fry than a rich starter.

alternatives.reason: Go for the Tandoori Broccoli Makhani from Rajshri Food if you're in the mood for a heavier, tomato based gravy and don't mind a longer ingredient list.

query: Broccoli, cashews, and mint. One of those melt-in-your-mouth Indian restaurant starters?

queries: Here is a melt-in-your-mouth Indian restaurant-style broccoli cashew starter recipe:
Here is a creamy Indian appetizer recipe using broccoli, cashews, and mint chutney:
Here is a restaurant-style broccoli cashew pepper fry starter recipe:

recommendation.headline: Malai Broccoli is exactly the melt-in-your-mouth Indian starter you're picturing, and the My Ginger Garlic Kitchen version uses your broccoli, cashews, and mint right in the marinade.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You already have the three ingredients that actually define this dish: broccoli florets, cashews blended into the creamy base, and mint worked into the marinade rather than just as a garnish. That's rarer than it sounds, most versions only add mint as a chutney afterthought. This one bakes to that golden, creamy, restaurant-style bite you're after.

missing_items.note: this is what makes the marinade cling and go golden, plain thick yogurt strained a bit works as a swap
adds richness and helps browning, you can skip it and just baste with a bit more oil or butter while baking

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the My Ginger Garlic Kitchen malai broccoli page start to finish for the marinade, baking time, and temperature since it's built around your exact three ingredients already.

recipe.title: Air fryer Malai Broccoli Tikka | Broccoli in Air fryer - Yummy tales of tummy

alternatives.reason: Pick this one if you'd rather air fry than bake and want the mint served as a chutney on the side instead of blended in.
Good backup if you're missing cheese entirely and want a version that leans more on a straightforward cashew paste marinade.

recipe.title: Air Fryer Malai Broccoli Recipe  - Fun FOOD Frolic

alternatives.reason: Another air fryer option if you want a faster cook time and don't mind mint being a dip rather than part of the marinade.
